answer:
The patient has hypocalcemia with an elevated parathyroid hormone level, indicating secondary hyperparathyroidism due to impaired blood supply to the parathyroid glands. The inferior thyroid artery, a branch of the thyrocervical trunk, is the main blood supply to the parathyroid glands and is commonly ligated during thyroidectomy. Therefore, damage to a branch of the thyrocervical trunk is the most likely cause.

\boxed{C}
